steps.title: Set equations equal to find intersection
steps.content:
When the parabola and line intersect, their $y$-values are equal. Setting $2x^2 - 13x + 24 = 3x + a$ and rearranging:
$$2x^2 - 16x + 24 - a = 0$$

steps.title: Apply discriminant condition for exactly one intersection
steps.content:
For the graphs to intersect at exactly one point (tangent), the quadratic must have exactly one solution. This occurs when the discriminant equals zero. With coefficients $a = 2$, $b = -16$, and $c = 24 - a$:
$$(-16)^2 - 4(2)(24-a) = 0$$
$$256 - 8(24-a) = 0$$
$$256 - 192 + 8a = 0$$
$$a = -8$$

steps.title: Solve for x with the value of a
steps.content:
Substituting $a = -8$ into our quadratic equation:
$$2x^2 - 16x + 32 = 0$$
$$x^2 - 8x + 16 = 0$$
$$(x - 4)^2 = 0$$
$$x = 4$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
